claim_chain: Trypanosomes must acquire nutrients while evading immune responses
Previous model proposed receptors are sequestered in the flagellar pocket to hide from immune effectors
We investigated distribution of GPI-anchored receptors on the cell surface
Both single and double GPI-anchored TfRs are present over the whole cell surface
Two other GPI-anchored receptors are also present over the whole cell surface
Mechanisms by which trypanosome receptors are protected from antibody-mediated attack are more complex than hiding in a pocket

typical_pattern: Context statement about pathogen survival strategy
Description of previous model or understanding
Statement of experimental findings that challenge or extend the model
Discussion of methodological differences that explain discrepancies
Interpretation of results in light of broader mechanisms

likely_priorities: Reconciliation with previous conflicting reports on receptor localization
Methodological rigor in cell handling and fixation protocols
Generalizability of findings across different receptor types
Mechanistic explanation for immune evasion if receptors are surface-exposed
likely_attacks: Discrepancy with established literature showing flagellar pocket localization
Potential artifacts from fixation protocol differences
Incomplete explanation of how surface-exposed receptors avoid immune attack
Limited mechanistic insight into retention or trafficking mechanisms
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper extensively discusses why experimental differences have arisen between their findings and previous reports, focusing on cell manipulation protocols before fixation. It states 'much of the previous work involved manipulations of the trypanosomes before determining either transferrin binding or TfR/HpHbR localisation' and 'providing time for the ligand bound receptors to be cleared from the cell surface'. The paper also acknowledges that 'The mechanisms by which trypanosome receptors are protected from antibody-mediated attack are more complex than hiding in a pocket', indicating awareness that their findings raise new mechanistic questions.

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, author names, or invented numbers were included. The profile is based solely on the provided abstract, discussion, and figure legends.
